data.title: Scott Bessent Says 'We Will Follow The Money' As US Sanctions $344M From Iran-Linked Crypto Wallets
data.description: Today, the US Treasury imposed sanctions on numerous crypto wallets connected to Iran, following the freezing of approximately $344 million in USDT linked to the same network by stablecoin issuer Tether. The freeze affected two TRX addresses holding roughly $213 million and $131 million each, conducted in cooperation with the Office of Foreign Assets Control and US law enforcement. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated, "We will track the funds that Tehran is desperately trying to move out of the country." This move is part of Iran's broader strategy of using crypto channels to evade sanctions. Since mid-March, Iran has been leveraging these methods to facilitate transactions and bypass restrictions.

data.title: The Chip Stock Rally That Broke History: SOXX Notches Record 17-Day Win Streak
data.description: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has broken a record by achieving 17 consecutive positive trading sessions, surpassing the previous high of 15 from 2014. This streak has led to a significant surge of approximately 42% in the SOX, marking its largest monthly gain since February 2000 during the dot-com boom. The SOXL is currently reaching all-time highs. The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) has experienced its best month in 25 years with a gain of over 30% in April. Similarly, the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) has seen its strongest monthly return since November 2003, with an increase of more than 25%. Traders utilizing Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares (SOXL) and Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bear 3X Shares (SOXS) have experienced extreme volatility. The complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Crypto PAC Fellowship withdraws $1.7M support from Texas AG for Senate candidacy amid reporting, per report.
data.description: A political action committee closely associated with cryptocurrency has disclosed contributing over $1.7 million to election authorities. The committee was initially planning to fund advertising for a candidate running for the Texas Senate but has since withdrawn their support.

data.title: Crypto industry sees increased bets on Bitcoin and Ether amid market rebound hopes, as institutions explore blockchain and lawmakers delay crypto regulations.
data.description: Experienced investors are increasing their investments in Bitcoin and Ether as the markets show signs of recovering. Meanwhile, institutions are exploring the use of blockchain technology and lawmakers in the US are delaying the implementation of new regulations regarding cryptocurrencies this week.
Billionaire Investor Cuts Tylenol Maker Kenvue Stake By 64%

data.title: FDA Flags High-Risk Recall For Tandem Diabetes Mobi Pumps
data.description: The FDA has issued a Class I recall for insulin pumps from Tandem Diabetes Care Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM), warning of serious injury or death if not corrected. The recall focuses on fixing devices in use, not removing them from the market. Tandem's insulin pumps are battery-operated and deliver basal and bolus insulin via a motor-driven system. The correction affects Tandem's Mobi insulin pumps.

data.title: Hospital Operator HCA Healthcare Flags Soft Respiratory Season As Volumes Lag
data.description: HCA Healthcare Inc., a leading hospital operator in the U.S., reported first-quarter 2026 revenues of $19.11 billion, marking a 4.3% increase from the previous year and closely aligned with the anticipated $19.10 billion. The company's adjusted earnings rose to $7.15 from $6.45 last year, meeting expectations. Within the same-facility metrics, there was a 0.9% increase in admissions and a 1.3% rise in equivalent admissions. Same-facility emergency room visits went up by 0.3%, while inpatient surgeries decreased by 0.3% and outpatient surgeries dropped by 1.7%. Revenue per equivalent admission in same facilities increased by 3.1%. HCA's adjusted EBITDA stood at $3.80 billion compared to $3.73 billion in the previous year.

data.title: Sam Appleton, Annie Fuller Win St. Anthony's Triathlon
data.description: Over the weekend, a multitude of athletes and volunteers flocked to St. Petersburg for Florida's picturesque race. The 43rd St. Anthony's Triathlon saw Sam Appleton and Annie Fuller emerging victorious on Sunday morning amidst the bustling event. The race attracted thousands of participants and supporters, solidifying its status as one of the most prominent sporting events in the area.

data.title: Coachella tests live entertainment's future with Google DeepMind AI, exploring artist tools and immersive experiences.
data.description: Coachella is exploring the use of artificial intelligence to create tools for artists, develop immersive digital environments, and preserve performances in 3D archives. These innovations have the potential to enhance the fan experience at the festival.

data.title: Litecoin post-attack update: Certain valid transactions unaffected, doubts raised about zero-day theory by some devs.
data.description: The Litecoin development team stated that the valid transactions within the affected blocks were not affected and are secure on the main chain.

data.title: Analyst notes 'Disbelief Rally' as Bitcoin hits $77,497, up 13% in April. Debate on rally as potential recovery or further decline.
data.description: In April, Bitcoin recorded a significant 13% increase in its value, reaching a trading price of $77,497. This surge has sparked debates among analysts who are split on interpreting the rally - some see it as a positive indicator of recovery, while others view it as a potential precursor to further decline.

data.title: Greater San Diego Science and Engineering Fair Students Win Big at the 75th California Science and Engineering Fair
data.description: The 75th California Science and Engineering Fair (CSEF) was held on April 11–12 at California Lutheran University in Thousand Oaks. The event gathered 900 top 1st place winners from regional competitions encompassing 58 counties in California.
Edwards Lifesciences Stock Surges On Heart Valve Devices Growth

data.title: A soldier unable to place bets on Polymarket due to Kalshi's KYC requirements after using classified intel.
data.description: A member of the U.S. military is facing charges for utilizing confidential military information to engage in betting on Polymarket. The individual in question was unable to successfully complete the Know Your Customer (KYC) verification process required by Kalshi.

data.title: Cardano Founder Partners With Google On New AI Privacy Blockchain 'Midnight'
data.description: Charles Hoskinson, the founder of Cardano (CRYPTO: ADA), orchestrated a significant deal with UK bank Monument, securing $250 million in tokenized deposits during the launch of his new privacy blockchain project, Midnight. This endeavor also involved partnerships with tech giants Google Cloud, MoneyGram, and Vodafone. Hoskinson highlighted the strategic importance of the $250 million Monument deal, emphasizing that compliance officers can utilize scripts to define permissible actions and asset interactions while adhering to UK regulations. He specifically praised Fahmy Syed for facilitating the deal, citing Syed's track record of forming over 100 partnerships within nine months at the Midnight Foundation. This collaboration establishes a foundation for future similar deals with financial institutions in the U.S. and Europe, as well as the Bank of England. Monument aims to occupy a space that Hoskinson refers to as "Web 2.5," blending aspects of traditional regulated business practices and cryptocurrency with a focus on rules and control. According to Hoskinson, there is currently no specialized blockchain tailored for this purpose. Midnight was developed to address this gap in the market.

data.title: Data at Heart Rhythm 2026 highlight key Boston Scientific therapies
data.description: Data released today by Boston Scientific Corporation (NYSE: BSX) showed positive results for FARAPULSE™ Pulsed Field Ablation and WATCHMAN™ LAAC Devices in late-breaking clinical trial sessions held in Chicago on April 26, 2026. The therapy demonstrated both safety and effectiveness, confirming its potential as a valuable treatment option. The information presented emphasizes the promising outcomes of the trials, reinforcing the credibility of these medical devices in the field.

data.title: Janet Yellen Keeps Fed Cut Hopes Alive As Iran War Roils Markets— 'If I Had To Write One Thing Down...'
data.description: During the HSBC Global Investment Summit in Hong Kong, former U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen expressed uncertainty about a potential Federal Reserve rate cut later this year. Yellen acknowledged the impact of the Iran war on oil-driven inflation, complicating the central bank's decisions. Despite rising inflation expectations, Yellen mentioned that there remains a possibility for a rate cut. She described the Iran conflict as creating a widespread supply shock affecting various sectors such as gasoline, LNG, fertilizers, food, and shipping costs. Yellen's insights suggest a cautious approach by the Fed, considering the ongoing economic challenges. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.
